source_pr.title: expression: refactor builtin function RAND to be compatible with MySQL
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
1. builtinRandSig/builtinRandWithSeedSig.randGen may cause data race when Project executing parallelly.
2. Results of rand(N) is not compatible with MySQL as https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/9387#discussion_r258768167 mentioned.

### What is changed and how it works?
1. Eager evaluate seed for rand() in getFunction.
2. Check whether args[0] is a constant during `getFunction`:
If so, return `builtinRandSig` rather than `builtinRandWithSeedSig`;
If not, evaluate seed each time Rand invoked.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

integration tests

Code changes

 - Has exported function/method change
 - Has exported variable/fields change

Side effects

N/A

Related changes

 - Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

symptoms: 1. builtinRandSig/builtinRandWithSeedSig.randGen may cause data race when Project executing parallelly.
failure_signature: 1. builtinRandSig/builtinRandWithSeedSig.randGen may cause data race when Project executing parallelly.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to concurrency races or unsynchronized shared state. Running in parallel with shared state can introduce races. This PR stabilizes it by: expression: refactor builtin function RAND to be compatible with MySQL.
fix_pattern: expression: refactor builtin function RAND to be compatible with MySQL

evidence: evidence: 1. builtinRandSig/builtinRandWithSeedSig.randGen may cause data race when Project executing parallelly.
